name: C.R.A.W.LAB (Controls, Robotics, and Automation)
relevance: Conducts research on controls, robotics, and automation with a focus on human interaction. Projects include autonomous surface vehicles (RoboBoat) and robot arms.

name: CAPE (Cajun Advanced Picosatellite Experiment)
relevance: An undergraduate student-run organization that researches, designs, and builds picosatellites (CubeSats) for NASA missions, including CAPE-3 and CAPE-4.

name: UL Lafayette/NASA Regional Application Center (RAC)
relevance: Partners with NASA to support space-related research, technology development, and remote sensing initiatives.

name: UL Lafayette Rocketry Team
relevance: A student team that participates in the NASA Student Launch competition, designing and launching high-powered rockets.

name: UL Lafayette Starshade Team
relevance: Designs and builds small-scale starshades for NASA to enhance exoplanet detection using inflatable truss systems.

name: IEEE UL Lafayette Student Branch
relevance: Includes a robotics competition team that has won regional competitions and provides hands-on engineering experience in robotics and electrical systems.

name: Institute for Materials Research & Innovation (IMRI) Robotics Lab
relevance: Focuses on the intersection of advanced materials and robotics, supporting research and innovation in robotic platforms.

name: Louisiana Immersive Technologies Enterprise (LITE)
relevance: Develops immersive and robotic technologies applied to aerospace training, simulation, and autonomous systems.

name: ASME UL Lafayette Student Chapter
relevance: Active student chapter involved in mechanical design and robotics competitions, often collaborating with other engineering groups.

name: Fluid Power Vehicle Team
relevance: A mechanical engineering competition team that designs and builds innovative vehicles using fluid power and controls.

name: AutoDrive Challenge II Team
relevance: Mentioned in relation to the university's AI journey and autonomous vehicle competitions.

name: SEDS (Students for the Exploration and Development of Space) UL Lafayette Chapter
relevance: Historically mentioned in engineering competition contexts; activity in 2026 requires verification.
